name: The Booker Prize
description: One of the world's most prestigious literary awards, given annually for the best novel written in English and published in the UK or Ireland. Open to writers of any nationality since 2014.
notes: Submitted by publishers, not authors directly.

name: International Booker Prize
description: Celebrates the best fiction translated into English and published in the UK or Ireland. The £50,000 prize is shared equally between author and translator. Short story collections eligible.
notes: Submitted by publishers. Translators equally recognised.

name: Pulitzer Prize for Fiction
description: America's most prestigious literary honour, awarded annually for a distinguished work of fiction by an American author, preferably dealing with American life.
notes: Must be American citizen. Publisher-submitted.

name: Bridport Prize — Short Story
description: One of the world's most respected writing competitions. Open to writers worldwide aged 16+. Max 5,000 words. Winners published in the annual Bridport anthology. Strong career-launching track record.
notes: Bursaries available for underrepresented writers.

name: Bridport Prize — Poetry
description: One of the most coveted poetry prizes in the English-speaking world. Up to 42 lines. Open to all writers aged 16+ worldwide. Winners published in the Bridport anthology.
notes: Bursaries available. Special prize for writers aged 60+.

name: Bridport Prize — Flash Fiction
description: 250 words max. Open to writers aged 16+ worldwide. One of the most competitive flash fiction prizes. Winners published in the annual Bridport anthology.
notes: Bursaries available for underrepresented writers.

name: Bridport Prize — Novel Award
description: The Peggy Chapman-Andrews First Novel Award. Submit 5,000–8,000 words from opening chapters plus a 300-word synopsis. A win or shortlist is one of the most powerful calling cards for literary agents.
notes: Agent connections provided. Must be significantly progressed on novel.

name: National Poetry Competition
description: Run by The Poetry Society since 1978, one of the world's most prestigious prizes for a single unpublished poem. Open to all poets worldwide aged 18+. Judged anonymously.
notes: Members receive discounted entries.

name: Bath Novel Award
description: International contest for unpublished novelists worldwide. Submit the first 5,000 words plus a one-page synopsis. Known for supporting emerging writers and connecting them to the publishing industry.
notes: Shortlisted entries receive manuscript feedback.

name: Scotiabank Giller Prize
description: Canada's most prestigious literary prize, awarded for the best Canadian novel or short story collection published in English. Publisher-submitted. One of the richest literary prizes in Canada.
notes: Authors must be Canadian citizens or permanent residents.

name: Miles Franklin Literary Award
description: Australia's most prestigious literary award for a novel that presents Australian life in any of its phases. Publisher-submitted. Winners include Patrick White, Tim Winton, and Richard Flanagan.
notes: Must present Australian life. Australian authors only.

name: Australian Book Review — Elizabeth Jolley Short Story Prize
description: A significant cash prize for the best unpublished short story. Open to Australian and international writers. 2,000–5,000 words. Winning and shortlisted stories published in Australian Book Review.
notes: Entries judged anonymously.

name: Carol Shields Prize for Fiction
description: One of the world's most valuable literary awards, honouring exceptional writing by women and non-binary writers in the US and Canada. Launched 2022 to address gender imbalance in literary recognition.
notes: Open to women and non-binary writers in USA and Canada.

name: Alpine Fellowship Writing Prize
description: Free-entry contest for the best philosophical-themed writing. Offers one of the largest free-entry prizes in creative writing. Open internationally.
notes: Theme changes annually. No entry fee.

name: John Pollard International Poetry Prize
description: Awarded to a debut full-length poetry collection published originally in English. Publishers submit four copies. One of the most valuable poetry prizes for debut collections.
notes: Submitted by publishers, not individual authors.

name: Rathbones Folio Prize
description: Awarded to the best work of literature in the English language, regardless of form or nationality. Nominated by Academy members. Celebrates literary merit across fiction, nonfiction, and poetry.
notes: Nominated by Folio Academy members. Open to all English-language works.

name: F(r)iction Short Story Contest
description: Seeks bold, original writing that challenges norms and takes creative risks. Emotional stories with fresh perspectives. No AI-generated work accepted. International entries welcome.
notes: No AI-generated content. Human-written only.

name: Yeovil Literary Prize
description: Open to anyone over 16. Five categories: Novel, Short Story, Poetry, Children's/YA Novel, and Writing Without Restrictions. Strong track record of supporting emerging writers.
notes: Multiple categories available.

name: Betty Trask Prize
description: For first novels in a 'traditional or romantic' style by Commonwealth citizens under 35. Can be published or unpublished. One of the few major prizes for debut novelists with age restrictions.
notes: Commonwealth citizens only. Author must be under 35.

name: Fish Publishing Short Story Prize
description: Internationally respected short story prize. Max 5,000 words. Open to all writers worldwide. Winners and shortlisted stories published in the Fish Anthology, a respected showcase for new voices.
notes: Winners published in Fish Anthology.

name: Fish Publishing Flash Fiction Prize
description: Free-entry flash fiction prize. Max 300 words. Open internationally. Top poem earns €1,000. Winners published in the Fish Anthology. A prestigious platform for writers of compressed fiction.
notes: Free entry. 300 words max.

name: Reedsy Short Story Contest
description: Weekly themed short story contest with rolling deadlines and free entry. Open internationally. Strong community of writers. A good entry point for emerging writers seeking regular competition practice.
notes: New prompt each week. No entry fee.

name: Aesthetica Creative Writing Award
description: Free-entry contest for poetry and short fiction. Winners published in Aesthetica Magazine and receive £1,000. Open internationally. Celebrates innovative and boundary-pushing creative writing.
notes: Free entry. Publication included.

name: Tom-Gallon Trust Award
description: Open to citizens of the UK, Commonwealth, or Republic of Ireland who have had at least one short story published or accepted. Max 5,000 words. Run by the Society of Authors.
notes: Must have at least one published short story. UK/Commonwealth/Ireland only.

name: Next Generation Short Story Awards
description: International short story competition with multiple genre categories. Stories must be no longer than 5,000 words. No sexual, political or hate speech content accepted. Open to international submissions.
notes: Multiple genre categories available.

name: Galley Beggar Press Short Story Prize
description: No entry fee prize run by the independent publisher Galley Beggar Press. Seeks extraordinary storytelling. Open internationally. One of the few free-entry prizes from a respected independent press.
notes: Free entry. Strong indie publisher connections.

name: Crime Writers' Association Debut Dagger
description: International competition for crime novelists writing in English. All crime subgenres welcome. Entrants may not have self-published fiction over 20,000 words. Win can lead to agent representation.
notes: All crime subgenres eligible including thriller, noir, cozy, suspense.

name: Rubery Book Award
description: Prestigious international competition specifically for independently published authors and small presses. Recognises quality books outside traditional publishing. No word count limits.
notes: Self-published or small press only. Free for short stories category.

name: Letter Review Prize
description: Free-entry competition awarding US$1,000 across multiple genres including short stories, poetry, and flash fiction. Open internationally. Rolling deadlines make it accessible throughout the year.
notes: Free entry. Rolling deadlines.

name: Orange Prize / Women's Prize for Fiction
description: Created to address gender bias in literary awards. Celebrates excellence, originality, and accessibility in women's writing. Open to women of any nationality writing in English. £30,000 prize.
notes: Open to women of any nationality writing in English.

name: Prime Minister's Literary Awards — Fiction
description: Australia's richest literary prize, with AU$80,000 for the winner and AU$5,000 for each shortlisted entry. Open to Australian citizens or permanent residents. First published in the eligible year. The prestige equivalent of Australia's Booker Prize.
notes: Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident. No entry fee.

name: Prime Minister's Literary Awards — Poetry
description: Australia's most valuable poetry prize, awarding AU$80,000 to the winning collection. One of six categories in Australia's most prestigious literary awards programme. No entry fee.
notes: Must be Australian citizen or permanent resident.

name: The Stella Prize
description: Australia's premier literary prize for women and non-binary writers. AU$60,000 for the winner of a book-length work of any genre — fiction, nonfiction, or poetry. Publisher-submitted. Established 2013 to address gender imbalance in Australian literary awards.
notes: Open to Australian women and non-binary writers. Publisher-submitted.

name: Richell Prize for Emerging Writers
description: Free-entry prize for unpublished emerging Australian writers of adult fiction or narrative non-fiction. Submit first three chapters plus synopsis. Winner receives AU$10,000 and a year's mentoring from a Hachette Australia publisher, with first option to publish. Track record of leading to publication deals.
notes: Must reside in Australia. Free to enter. Does not require a completed manuscript.

name: Penguin Literary Prize
description: Free-entry prize from Penguin Random House Australia for unpublished adult literary fiction. Open to Australian residents 18+. Complete manuscripts only (up to 100,000 words). Winner receives a publishing contract — one of Australia's most direct pathways to major publication.
notes: Australian residents only. Free entry. Complete manuscript required.

name: Newcastle Poetry Prize
description: Australia's largest open poetry prize, awarding AU$15,000 for the best single poem. Open internationally. Winning poem published in the annual anthology alongside shortlisted entries. A cornerstone prize that has launched major Australian poetry careers.
notes: Open to all poets worldwide. AU$10 per poem.

name: Overland Neilma Sidney Short Story Prize
description: Supported by the Malcolm Robertson Foundation and named after novelist Neilma Gantner. Seeks powerful short fiction up to 3,000 words on an annual theme. Open internationally. Winner receives AU$5,000 and publication in Overland — one of Australia's most respected literary journals since 1954.
notes: Subscriber discount available. Theme changes annually. Open internationally.

name: Peter Carey Short Story Award
description: Free-entry short story prize open to all Australian residents. Stories between 2,000 and 3,000 words. Winner and runner-up both published in Overland literary journal. Named after the two-time Booker Prize winner. Top two stories guaranteed publication.
notes: Free entry. Australian residents only. Top two stories published.

name: AWC Furious Fiction
description: Monthly free-entry flash fiction contest run by the Australian Writers' Centre. Stories must be 500 words max and written within a weekend using set criteria. Open internationally. A beloved entry point for flash fiction writers with a large, supportive community.
notes: Stories written within 55 hours using monthly criteria. Free. International.

name: Sargeson Prize
description: New Zealand's largest and most prestigious short story prize. Free to enter. Named after celebrated NZ writer Frank Sargeson. Open to NZ citizens or permanent residents. Up to 5,000 words in the Open Division. Judged blind by high-profile NZ authors. Established 2019 by author Catherine Chidgey.
notes: NZ citizens and permanent residents only. Free entry. No simultaneous submissions.

name: Bristol Short Story Prize
description: Internationally respected short story prize run since 2008. Up to 4,000 words. Open to writers worldwide. All shortlisted writers receive £50 and publication in the Bristol Short Story anthology. Past judges include Jan Carson and Peace Adzo Medie. Typically receives around 1,800 submissions.
notes: All shortlisted writers published and receive £50. International entries welcome.

name: The Moth Short Story Prize
description: Prestigious international prize from Ireland's celebrated literary magazine. Up to 3,000 words. Open to anyone over 16 worldwide. Winning story published in the Irish Times summer fiction series. 2nd and 3rd prizes published in the Irish Times online. Past judges include Evie Wyld.
notes: Winning story published in the Irish Times. 2nd and 3rd also published online.

name: Mslexia Novel Competition
description: Open to women and non-binary writers worldwide writing in English. Submit the first 10,000 words of an unpublished novel plus a synopsis. Winner receives £5,000 plus pitch training and introductions to agents and editors at a London Talent Party — one of the best prizes for connecting debut novelists with the UK publishing industry.
notes: Open to women and non-binary writers worldwide. Winner also receives mentoring.

name: Mslexia Short Story Competition
description: Open to women and non-binary writers worldwide. Up to 2,200 words. Winner and finalists published in Mslexia and the Best Women's Short Fiction anthology. One of the UK's most well-regarded short story competitions for women writers.
notes: Women and non-binary writers only. Winner published in Mslexia magazine.

name: BBC National Short Story Award
description: One of the world's most prestigious and valuable prizes for a single short story. Open to UK writers submitted via publisher or agent. Winner receives £15,000. Shortlisted stories broadcast on BBC Radio 4 and published in an annual anthology. Established 2005.
notes: UK writers only. Submitted via publisher or agent. Winning stories broadcast on BBC Radio 4.

name: Fish Publishing Poetry Prize
description: Respected international poetry prize. Open to all poets worldwide. Winners and shortlisted poems published in the annual Fish Anthology. Judged anonymously. Maximum poem length of 300 lines. Entry includes consideration for publication.
notes: Winners published in Fish Anthology. Judged anonymously.

name: CBC Short Story Prize
org: CBC Books / Canada Council for the Arts
description: Canada's premier free-entry short story prize. Open to Canadian citizens or permanent residents 18+. Up to 2,500 words. Winner receives CA$6,000 and winning and shortlisted stories broadcast on CBC Radio and published on CBC Books. One of the most visible platforms for Canadian fiction writers.
notes: Canadian citizens or permanent residents only. Free entry.

name: CBC Poetry Prize
org: CBC Books / Canada Council for the Arts
description: Canada's most prestigious free-entry poetry prize. Open to Canadian citizens or permanent residents 18+. Winning and shortlisted poems published on CBC Books and broadcast on CBC Radio. One of the most prominent national platforms for Canadian poets.
notes: Canadian citizens or permanent residents only. Free entry.

name: RBC Bronwen Wallace Award — Short Fiction
description: Prestigious award for emerging Canadian writers of short fiction who are published in literary journals but not yet in book form. Up to 2,500 words. Free to enter. Winner receives CA$10,000 and mentoring. Finalists invited to Toronto literary career development events. Strong track record of launching book deals.
notes: Must be Canadian citizen/resident, published in literary journal, but unpublished in book form.

name: RBC Bronwen Wallace Award — Poetry
description: Top award for emerging Canadian poets published in literary journals but not yet in book form. Established 1994 in honour of poet and teacher Bronwen Wallace. Free to enter. Winner receives CA$10,000, mentoring, and career development support from the Writers' Trust of Canada.
notes: Canadian citizens/residents only. Must be published in journals but not yet in book form.

name: Griffin Poetry Prize
org: Griffin Trust for Excellence in Poetry
description: One of the world's most valuable prizes for a single poetry collection, with CA$65,000 each for best Canadian and best International collection. Publisher-submitted. Has transformed the global standing of poetry. Past winners include Les Murray, Anne Carson, and Terrance Hayes.
notes: Publisher submission. Two categories: Canadian and International collection.

name: Governor General's Literary Awards — Fiction
description: Canada's oldest and most prestigious literary award, established 1936. Recognises the best Canadian fiction in English. Publisher-submitted. CA$25,000 for the winner. Winning book receives a specially bound copy presented by the Governor General.
notes: Canadian citizens, permanent residents, and landed immigrants only. Publisher-submitted.

name: PEN/Faulkner Award for Fiction
description: One of America's most prestigious fiction awards, recognising the best published work of American fiction (novel, short story collection, or novella). Judged entirely by writers. Finalists each receive US$5,000. Ceremony held annually at Washington DC's Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library.
notes: Author must be American. Works of fiction published in the calendar year.

name: Iowa Short Fiction Award
description: Prestigious annual prize for a debut short story collection. Open to writers who have not previously published a full-length fiction manuscript. Winner receives publication with the University of Iowa Press and national distribution. Has launched the careers of many acclaimed short story writers.
notes: Open to writers who have not published a full-length fiction book. US writers eligible.

name: American Short Fiction Prize
prize: US$1,000 + publication in American Short Fiction
description: Prize from one of America's most distinguished literary journals. Stories up to 12,000 words. Open internationally. Winner receives US$1,000 and publication. American Short Fiction has published work by Joyce Carol Oates, Mary Gaitskill, and Justin Torres. Judged anonymously.
notes: Open internationally. Publication in American Short Fiction included.

name: Lorian Hemingway Short Story Competition
entryFeeNote: US$15 (up to 2,500 words) / US$20 (up to 5,000 words)
description: Established 1981 by the granddaughter of Ernest Hemingway, this prestigious competition has over 40 years of history. Focuses on emerging voices in short fiction. Open internationally. Up to 5,000 words. All manuscripts reviewed by Lorian Hemingway personally.
notes: All submissions reviewed by Lorian Hemingway. 43+ years of history.

name: Iowa Review Awards — Poetry
prize: US$1,500 + publication in The Iowa Review
description: Annual contest from one of America's most distinguished literary journals, founded at the University of Iowa in 1969. Open internationally. Submit up to 3 poems (8 pages max). Winner receives US$1,500 and publication in The Iowa Review, a major credential in American poetry.
notes: Open internationally. Submit up to 3 poems. 50+ year history.

name: Bath Flash Fiction Award
description: One of the world's most reputable flash fiction prizes. Up to 300 words. Open internationally. Two rounds per year with £1,000 first prize each round. All shortlisted entries published in the award anthology. Respected for rigorous anonymous judging by established flash fiction writers.
notes: Two rounds: spring and autumn. All shortlisted work published in anthology.

name: Victorian Premier's Literary Award — Fiction
description: Part of Australia's richest state-based literary prize suite. Category winners receive $25,000 and compete for the Victorian Prize for Literature ($100,000). Administered by the Wheeler Centre. Past winners include Helen Garner, Richard Flanagan and Tom Keneally.
notes: Submitted by publishers. Must be published in the preceding calendar year. Australian citizens and permanent residents only.

name: Victorian Premier's Literary Award — Poetry
description: One of Australia's richest poetry prizes, part of the Victorian Premier's Literary Awards suite. The category winner receives $25,000 and competes for the overall Victorian Prize for Literature ($100,000). The 2026 winner was Evelyn Araluen for The Rot.
notes: Submitted by publishers. Published poetry collections only.

name: Victorian Premier's Literary Award — Unpublished Manuscript
description: Open to Victorian residents for an unpublished fiction manuscript — novel or story collection. Winner receives $15,000 plus a two-week creative residency at McCraith House on the Mornington Peninsula. Previous winners include Graeme Simsion (The Rosie Project) and Jane Harper (The Dry).
notes: Open to Victorian residents only. Unpublished manuscripts only — novel or story collection. Entries via the Wheeler Centre website.

name: NSW Premier's Literary Award — Christina Stead Prize for Fiction
org: State Library of NSW / Create NSW
description: Australia's longest-running state literary award. The Christina Stead Prize, worth $40,000, is the flagship fiction prize of the NSW Premier's Literary Awards. Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents. Past winners include Peter Carey, Michelle de Kretser and Helen Garner.
notes: Australian citizens and permanent residents only. Works must be published in the eligible period. Total prize pool across all NSW awards is $320,000.

name: NSW Premier's Literary Award — Kenneth Slessor Prize for Poetry
org: State Library of NSW / Create NSW
description: One of Australia's most significant poetry prizes, awarded annually as part of the NSW Premier's Literary Awards for a published collection or long poem. Named for celebrated Australian poet Kenneth Slessor. Part of a $320,000 total prize pool.
notes: Australian citizens and permanent residents only. Works published in the eligible period (Oct to Sep of prior year).

name: NSW Premier's Literary Award — UTS Glenda Adams Award for New Writing
org: State Library of NSW / Create NSW
description: A $10,000 prize within the NSW Premier's Literary Awards for debut fiction — the best first published novel or story collection by an Australian writer. Named for acclaimed Australian-American author Glenda Adams. Entries via the same process as all NSW Literary Awards.
notes: Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents. Debut fiction only — must be a first published book.

name: Queensland Literary Awards — Fiction
description: Part of one of Australia's most generous literary award suites, administered by the State Library of Queensland. Open to all Australian citizens and permanent residents. Covers fiction, poetry, non-fiction and emerging writers across 14 categories.
notes: Australian citizens and permanent residents. Books published in the eligible period. Free to enter.

name: Queensland Literary Awards — Poetry
description: A flagship poetry prize within the Queensland Literary Awards. Open to Australian citizens and permanent residents for a published poetry collection. One of Australia's most significant poetry prizes with generous prize money across 14 total categories.
notes: Australian citizens and permanent residents. Published collections only. Free to enter.

name: Queensland Literary Awards — David Unaipon Award (Unpublished Indigenous Writing)
description: Awarded to the best unpublished manuscript by an Aboriginal or Torres Strait Islander writer who has not yet had a book published. The winner receives $15,000 and a publishing contract with University of Queensland Press. One of the most important prizes in Australian Indigenous literature.
notes: Open to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander writers only. Unpublished manuscripts. Winner published by University of Queensland Press.

name: ABR Peter Porter Poetry Prize
description: Named for acclaimed Australian-born British poet Peter Porter, this prize is awarded annually by Australian Book Review for the best unpublished poem. Open internationally. One of Australia's most prestigious and closely-watched poetry prizes. The winning poem is published in ABR.
notes: Open internationally. Entry requires an ABR subscription (from $100). Check website for current cycle opening dates.

name: Griffith Review Emerging Voices Competition
description: An annual competition for emerging Australian and New Zealand writers of fiction and creative non-fiction. Up to four winners share a $20,000 prize pool and are published in Griffith Review. Open to writers with no more than one published book. Stories of 3,500–5,000 words.
notes: Open to Australian and New Zealand citizens and permanent residents. Emerging writers only (maximum one published book). Entries close April 2026.

name: Helen Anne Bell Poetry Bequest Award
description: Australia's richest poetry prize and one of its most prestigious, awarded annually to celebrate women poets. Worth $40,000. Past winners include Evelyn Araluen, Judith Beveridge, and Gig Ryan. Administered by the State Library of NSW.
notes: Open to women poets only. Publisher nomination. Check website for current cycle dates.

name: Voss Literary Prize
description: Awarded by the Sydney Morning Herald to the best literary novel published in Australia. Named for Patrick White's celebrated novel Voss. One of Australia's most prestigious annual fiction prizes, judged by a panel of critics and writers.
notes: Publisher nomination. Check website for current cycle. Named for Patrick White's novel Voss.

name: Indie Book Awards — Fiction
description: Voted by Australia's independent booksellers, the Indie Book Awards are among the most commercially significant prizes in Australian publishing. The fiction winner — and debut fiction winner — are strong indicators of bookseller endorsement and typically see significant sales uplift.
notes: Publisher submission. Voted by Australian independent booksellers. Strong commercial impact.

name: Griffith University — Queensland University of Technology Prize for Literature
description: A well-regarded annual prize for unpublished fiction run by Queensland University of Technology's Creative Writing program. Open to Australian and New Zealand writers. Shortlisted works receive publication consideration and mentoring opportunities.
notes: Check website for current cycle dates. Open to AU and NZ writers.

name: Meanjin Dinny O'Hearn Fellowship
description: A fellowship and prize from Meanjin — one of Australia's oldest and most prestigious literary journals — for emerging writers of fiction and essays. Publication in Meanjin is one of the most coveted credits in Australian literary writing.
notes: Check Meanjin website for current fellowship and prize cycle dates.

name: Overland Judith Wright Poetry Prize
description: An annual prize for an unpublished poem from Overland, one of Australia's most important literary and political journals. Named for poet Judith Wright. Winning and shortlisted poems are published in Overland. Open to Australian and NZ writers.
notes: Check Overland website for current cycle opening dates. Open to Australian and NZ writers.

name: Wheeler Centre Next Chapter Fellowship
description: A prestigious fellowship for Australian writers of underrepresented backgrounds to complete a work of literary fiction or narrative non-fiction. Provides $20,000 plus mentoring from major publishers. Previous recipients include Evelyn Araluen and Micaela Sahhar, both of whom went on to win the Victorian Prize for Literature.
notes: Targeted at writers from underrepresented backgrounds. Check Wheeler Centre website for current application cycle.

name: Ockham NZ Book Awards — Jann Medlicott Acorn Prize for Fiction
description: New Zealand's largest cash book prize and the country's premier literary honour for fiction, awarded annually at the Auckland Writers Festival. Open to novels and story collections by New Zealand writers. Winners include Catherine Chidgey (twice) and Emily Perkins.
notes: Publisher submission only. Open to New Zealand writers. Administered by the NZ Book Awards Trust.

name: Ockham NZ Book Awards — Mary and Peter Biggs Award for Poetry
description: New Zealand's premier poetry prize, awarded annually at the Auckland Writers Festival. Open to published poetry collections by New Zealand writers. A key marker of excellence in New Zealand poetry.
notes: Publisher submission only. New Zealand writers only.

name: Ockham NZ Book Awards — Best First Book (Fiction)
description: The Mātātuhi Foundation Best First Book Award for Fiction is awarded alongside the main Ockham Awards for the debut novel or story collection of highest literary merit by a New Zealand writer. One of the most career-defining debut prizes in New Zealand literature.
notes: Publisher submission only. Must be a debut work of fiction by a New Zealand writer.

name: Ockham NZ Book Awards — Best First Book (Poetry)
description: The Mātātuhi Foundation Best First Book Award for Poetry, awarded for the debut poetry collection of highest literary merit by a New Zealand writer. A landmark debut prize that has launched many of New Zealand's most celebrated contemporary poets.
notes: Publisher submission only. Must be a debut poetry collection by a New Zealand writer.

name: National Flash Fiction Day NZ Competition
description: An annual free-to-enter competition celebrating flash fiction in New Zealand, run each June as part of National Flash Fiction Day. Open to New Zealand writers of all experience levels. Youth category also available. Winners published in the NFFD anthology.
notes: New Zealand writers only. Free to enter. Held annually in June. Both open and youth divisions.

name: Takahē Short Story Competition
description: An annual short story competition run by Takahē, a respected New Zealand literary magazine supporting emerging and established writers and poets. Winners are published in Takahē. Open to New Zealand writers.
notes: Check Takahē website for current cycle opening and closing dates.

name: Takahē Monica Taylor Poetry Prize
description: An annual poetry prize run by Takahē, one of New Zealand's most established literary magazines. Open to New Zealand writers. Winning and shortlisted poems are published in Takahē. Named for founding editor Monica Taylor.
notes: Open to New Zealand writers. Entries open June and close September annually.

name: Prime Minister's Award for Literary Achievement — Fiction
description: New Zealand's most prestigious literary award, recognising distinguished achievement by a New Zealand writer of fiction. The prize — worth NZ$60,000 — is awarded to a body of work rather than a single book. Cannot be applied for; writers are nominated by a judging panel.
notes: By nomination only — cannot self-apply. Recognises a substantial body of work.

name: Prime Minister's Award for Literary Achievement — Poetry
description: New Zealand's highest literary honour for poetry. Awarded to a New Zealand poet for a distinguished body of work. The prize is worth NZ$60,000 and is administered by Creative New Zealand. Cannot be applied for; awarded by an expert judging panel.
notes: By nomination only — cannot self-apply. Recognises a substantial body of poetic work.

name: Landfall Essay Competition
description: An annual essay competition from Landfall — New Zealand's oldest and most prestigious literary journal, published since 1947. Open to New Zealand writers for essays on any topic. Publication in Landfall is one of the most coveted credits in NZ literary writing.
notes: Check Landfall website for current cycle. Open to New Zealand writers. Free to enter.

name: WriteMentor Novel & Picture Book Award
description: An annual award for unagented, unpublished writers of children's, YA and adult fiction, run in partnership with Darley Anderson Agency. Now in its eighth year with a strong track record — more than 100 alumni have secured agent representation or publishing deals. Three categories: Picture Books, Children's/YA, and Adult. Concessions available for low-income entrants.
notes: Open internationally to unagented, unpublished writers. Entry with editorial feedback available for £24. 30 free concession entries available per year.

name: An Post Irish Book Awards — Irish Novel of the Year
description: Ireland's most prestigious and popular book awards, voted by the public and judged by a panel. The Irish Novel of the Year is the flagship prize. Winners enjoy enormous sales uplift and national media attention. Past winners include Sally Rooney, Colm Tóibín and Donal Ryan.
notes: Publisher submission only. Open to Irish authors or books published in Ireland. Public vote determines shortlist; judging panel selects winner.

name: An Post Irish Book Awards — Poetry Collection of the Year
description: The leading Irish poetry prize, awarded as part of the An Post Irish Book Awards — Ireland's most prominent book prize suite. Open to poetry collections by Irish authors or books published in Ireland. Voted by the public with a judging panel selecting the winner.
notes: Publisher submission only. Irish authors or books published in Ireland.

name: RTÉ Short Story Competition (Francis MacManus Award)
description: One of Ireland's longest-established and most significant literary prizes, run by RTÉ Radio 1 in honour of writer and broadcaster Francis MacManus. Free to enter, with €5,000 for first place, €4,000 for second, €3,000 for third, and seven runners-up prizes of €300 each. Winning stories are broadcast on RTÉ Radio 1. Open to Irish writers worldwide.
notes: Free to enter. Open to writers living in Ireland and Irish writers around the world. Stories must be 1,800–2,000 words. Entries in English or Irish accepted.

name: Irish Writers Centre Debut Novel Competition
description: The Irish Writers Centre's flagship annual initiative gives unpublished novelists the opportunity to pitch their novels to world-class publishers and literary agents. The competition is open internationally and winners receive direct access to leading publishing figures. A strong track record of launching debut careers.
notes: Open internationally. Unpublished novels only. Check the Irish Writers Centre website for exact submission window dates.

name: Cúirt New Writing Prize
description: An annual prize for emerging writers run by the prestigious Cúirt International Festival of Literature in Galway. Four categories: short fiction, poetry, essay, and Irish language poetry/fiction. Winners receive €500 and an invitation to read at the Cúirt festival. Judged by major writers — 2026 judges include Eimear McBride (fiction) and Susannah Dickey (poetry). Open internationally.
notes: Open internationally. Writers must not have published a full collection in their entered category. Subsidised entries available on application.

name: Southword Chapbook Competition
description: An annual competition for poetry chapbooks run by the Munster Literature Centre and Southword Editions, launched at the Cork International Poetry Festival. Two winners selected — one debut, one established poet — each receiving €1,000 and publication. Up to 25 entrants listed as 'highly commended'.
notes: Open internationally. Submit 16–24 page poetry chapbooks. Winners launched at Cork International Poetry Festival.

name: Seamus Heaney Award for New Writing
org: Arts Council of Northern Ireland / Seamus Heaney HomePlace
description: A prestigious prize for new writing in poetry or short fiction named for Nobel laureate Seamus Heaney, run by the Seamus Heaney HomePlace in Bellaghy. Open to writers from Ireland and Northern Ireland. One of the most distinguished literary honours for new writing on the island of Ireland.
notes: Open to writers from Ireland and Northern Ireland. Check website for current cycle dates.

name: Gregory O'Donoghue International Poetry Competition
description: A major annual international poetry prize run by the Munster Literature Centre (publishers of Southword journal) and named for Cork poet Gregory O'Donoghue. Open to all writers worldwide. Winner and shortlisted poems published in Southword.
notes: Open internationally. Poems must be unpublished. Deadline typically November.

name: Writers' Trust Atwood Gibson Prize for Fiction
description: Canada's richest prize for a novel or short story collection, renamed in 2021 in honour of Margaret Atwood and Graeme Gibson. Awarded annually at the Writers' Trust Awards ceremony. Open to Canadian citizens and permanent residents. Finalists each receive C$6,000.
notes: Publisher submission only. Canadian citizens and permanent residents only.

name: Writers' Trust Hilary Weston Prize for Nonfiction
description: Canada's most prestigious and richest prize for literary nonfiction, awarded by the Writers' Trust of Canada. Open to Canadian citizens and permanent residents for a work of literary nonfiction. Finalists each receive C$6,000.
notes: Publisher submission only. Canadian citizens and permanent residents.

name: Governor General's Literary Award — English Fiction
description: Canada's oldest and most prestigious literary prize, administered by the Canada Council for the Arts. Awarded annually since 1936 across multiple categories. The English Fiction prize, worth C$25,000, recognises the best Canadian novel or short story collection published in English.
notes: Publisher submission only. Canadian citizens and permanent residents.

name: Governor General's Literary Award — English Poetry
description: One of Canada's most distinguished poetry prizes, awarded annually since 1981 for the best English-language poetry collection by a Canadian author. Part of the Governor General's Literary Awards suite, administered by the Canada Council for the Arts.
notes: Publisher submission only. Canadian citizens and permanent residents.

name: Trillium Book Award
description: Ontario's premier literary prize, awarded annually to the best book written by an Ontario author. Covers fiction, non-fiction and poetry. The prize recognises books of exceptional quality and outstanding contribution to English-language Canadian literature. Past winners include Alice Munro and Margaret Atwood.
notes: Open to Ontario-based authors. Publisher submission. Finalists receive C$2,500 each.

name: Amazon Canada First Novel Award
description: Canada's richest prize exclusively for debut novels, awarded annually to the best first novel by a Canadian author. The winner receives C$60,000 and finalists each receive C$6,000. One of the most career-defining prizes available to a debut Canadian novelist.
notes: Publisher submission only. Canadian citizens and permanent residents. Debut novels only.

name: CBC Short Story Prize
description: A free-to-enter annual prize from CBC Books for an unpublished short story by a Canadian writer. The winner receives C$6,000, publication on CBC Books, and a two-week writing residency at the Banff Centre. Four finalists each receive C$1,000. 2026 judges include Maria Reva.
notes: Free to enter. Canadian citizens and permanent residents. Stories must be 1,200–2,500 words. Submissions open September 2026.

name: Malahat Review Far Horizons Award
description: A biennial award from Canada's oldest literary magazine for emerging writers who have not yet published a full-length book. Alternates annually between poetry and fiction. Open internationally. Winners and finalists published in The Malahat Review.
notes: Open internationally. For writers who have not published a full-length book. Check website for current year's category (alternates poetry/fiction).

name: Writers' Trust Journey Prize
org: Writers' Trust of Canada / McClelland & Stewart
description: Canada's most prestigious prize for an emerging short story writer, funded by James A. Michener's royalties from his novel Journey. Stories are nominated by Canadian literary journals rather than self-submitted. The winner receives C$10,000 and publication in the annual Journey Prize Stories anthology. A major launchpad for Canadian fiction writers.
notes: Cannot be self-submitted. Stories must be nominated by Canadian literary journals where they are first published.

name: Carol Shields Prize for Fiction
description: One of the world's largest prizes for fiction by women and non-binary writers from the US and Canada, established in 2022. The winner receives US$150,000 — making it one of the richest literary prizes in North America. Open to novels and story collections.
notes: Open to women and non-binary writers who are US or Canadian citizens or permanent residents. Publisher submission only.

name: National Book Award — Fiction
description: One of America's most prestigious literary honours, awarded annually since 1950. Open to US citizens for a novel or story collection published in the US. Past winners include Colson Whitehead, Jonathan Franzen, and Don DeLillo. Finalists each receive $1,000 and significant industry recognition.
notes: Publisher submission only. US citizens only.

name: National Book Award — Poetry
description: One of the most prestigious honours in American poetry, awarded annually since 1950 to a US citizen for a poetry collection published in the US. Past winners include Louise Glück, Frank Bidart, and Terrance Hayes.
notes: Publisher submission only. US citizens only.

name: PEN/Robert W. Bingham Prize for Debut Fiction
description: Awarded by PEN America for an exceptionally talented debut fiction writer whose work represents distinguished literary achievement. One of the most significant debut prizes in American publishing. Open to US citizens and permanent residents. Finalists receive $1,000.
notes: Publisher submission. US citizens and permanent residents.

name: Ploughshares Emerging Writer's Contest
description: An annual contest from Ploughshares — one of America's most respected and widely read literary magazines — for writers who have not yet published a full-length book. Open internationally. Winners receive $2,000, publication, and a conversation with Aevitas Creative Management literary agency. Includes fiction, poetry, and creative non-fiction.
notes: Open internationally. For writers who have not published a full-length book. Entry includes a one-year Ploughshares subscription.

name: Missouri Review Jeffrey E. Smith Editors' Prize
description: An annual prize from The Missouri Review — one of America's most influential literary journals — for the best unpublished fiction, poetry, and essay. Open internationally. Winners receive $5,000 and publication in TMR. One of the most respected contest opportunities in American literary publishing.
notes: Open internationally. Separate categories for fiction, poetry, and essay. Check website for exact submission window.

name: Iowa Review Awards
description: Annual awards from one of America's most prestigious literary journals — published since 1970 by the University of Iowa. Three categories: poetry, fiction, and creative nonfiction. Winners receive $1,500 and runners-up $750. All winners published in the December issue. Open internationally.
notes: Open internationally. Accepts submissions during January only. Fiction up to 25 pages; poetry up to 10 pages.

name: Flannery O'Connor Award for Short Fiction
description: One of America's most distinguished prizes for a short story collection manuscript, published by the University of Georgia Press since 1983. Open internationally for manuscripts of 150–300 pages. One of the very few major prizes for unpublished story collections — a significant opportunity for short fiction writers.
notes: Open internationally. Unpublished collections of 150–300 pages. Submissions open April–May annually.

name: National Poetry Series
description: One of the most prestigious open competitions in American poetry, selecting five full-length poetry manuscripts annually for publication by major US publishers (including Penguin, Copper Canyon Press, and others). Each winner receives $10,000 and a publishing contract. Judges include leading American poets.
notes: Open to US residents only. Full-length poetry manuscripts. Entries accepted in January only.

name: Yale Series of Younger Poets
description: America's oldest poetry prize, awarded annually since 1919 by Yale University Press for a debut poetry collection. The prize has launched the careers of some of America's most celebrated poets, including Adrienne Rich and W.S. Merwin. Open to US citizens and residents who have not published a full poetry collection.
notes: US citizens and residents only. Debut collection only. Submissions open October–November annually.

name: Poets & Writers/Barnes & Noble Writers for Writers Award
description: Awarded annually by Poets & Writers to an author who has given generously to other writers — through mentorship, teaching, or advocacy. Cannot be applied for; recipients are selected by a judging committee. One of the most respected awards in American literary culture.
notes: By nomination only. Cannot be self-applied. Recognises contribution to the writing community.

name: BOMB Magazine Poetry Contest
description: An annual poetry contest from BOMB — one of New York's most respected arts and culture magazines, known for its artist-to-artist interviews and experimental literary content. Open internationally. The winner receives $1,000 and publication in BOMB's fall issue. The 2026 judge is National Book Award finalist Richard Siken.
notes: Open internationally. Check BOMB website for exact submission window and judge announcement.

name: AWG Emerging Writers' Awards
entryFeeNote: Free — AWG Associate or Student membership required
description: Australia's most important entry-level screen competition. Open to AWG Associate and Student members with an unproduced script across film, television, theatre, audio, and interactive. Shortlisted scripts are added to the AWG Pathways Showcase, giving them direct exposure to producers and development executives.
notes: AWG membership required. Closes 7 April 2026.

name: AWG Monte Miller Awards
description: Annual AWG award for unproduced scripts in two categories: Long Form (over 30 minutes) and Short Form (under 30 minutes), across all genres and formats. Winners announced at a dedicated Melbourne event. One of Australia's most respected peer-judged screenplay awards.
notes: Open to AWG members only. Entries typically open March.

name: AWG John Hinde Award
description: Recognises the best science-fiction writing for feature film, television, web series, audio, and interactive media. Funded by a bequest from the late Australian film critic John Hinde. Two categories: best produced script and best unproduced script.
notes: Closes 7 April 2026. Open to AWG members.

name: QWC Scriptable Program
description: National development program for emerging Australian screenwriters. Accepts unproduced scripts across feature film, television, web series, and short film. Successful applicants receive one-on-one mentorship from a matched screen industry professional, QWC staff consultation, and networking opportunities.
notes: Applications open nationally. Check QWC site for current dates.

name: Australian International Screenwriting Awards (AISA)
description: International screenplay competition based in Australia, open to screenwriters worldwide. Accepts feature scripts, shorts, and TV pilots. Emphasises detailed reader feedback alongside recognition, and focuses on both commercial and artistic merit. Writers retain all rights.
notes: Open to international entrants. Previously optioned/produced scripts not eligible.

name: PAGE International Screenwriting Awards
entryFeeNote: USD $40 early / $55 standard (feature); short scripts discounted
description: One of Hollywood's most respected screenplay contests, judged by working producers, agents, managers, and development executives. Ten genre categories including Action/Adventure, Comedy, Drama, Horror, Sci-Fi, and more. Open to writers worldwide — past winners have secured representation, option deals, and film/TV production.
notes: Open internationally. Tiered deadlines with early-bird pricing.

name: Final Draft Big Break Screenwriting Contest
entryFeeNote: USD $60 features; TV pilots and shorts priced separately
description: Presented by the makers of the industry-standard screenwriting software, Big Break accepts feature films, TV pilots (hour-long and half-hour), and short scripts. Open internationally — past entrants include writers from Australia, NZ, UK, Canada, and Ireland. Winners gain industry introductions, coaching, and software prizes.
notes: Open to international writers. Spec scripts for existing TV series not eligible.

name: Script Pipeline Screenwriting Contest
description: One of the longest-running U.S. screenplay contests, focused on connecting writers with representation. Quarterfinalists and above receive personalised development support and circulation to leading industry executives. Over $8 million in spec sales by competition alumni. Also runs a separate TV Writing Contest and Pitch Contest.
notes: Open internationally. TV Writing Contest runs separately. Early deadlines offer reduced fees.

name: Austin Film Festival Screenplay Competition
description: Known as the 'writer's festival', AFF runs one of the world's largest screenwriting conferences alongside its competition. Categories include feature, short, pilot, and playwriting. Finalists gain direct access to Hollywood agents, managers, and producers through panels and networking events. Open to writers worldwide.
notes: Open internationally. Multiple categories. Conference access for finalists is a major draw.

name: NYC Midnight Screenwriting Challenge
description: A timed, prompt-based screenwriting competition running three rounds. Writers receive a genre, subject, and character assignment and must write an original short screenplay within days. Popular with Australian, UK, NZ, and Irish writers. Judges provide feedback on every submission and writers retain all rights.
notes: Annual competition. 2026 edition already underway — registration opens late 2026 for 2027 round.

name: HollyShorts Screenplay Contest
description: Part of the annual HollyShorts Film Festival (Aug 13–23, 2026), this contest accepts short screenplays up to 30 pages and TV pilots up to 50 pages. Winners gain exposure to festival industry attendees and potential production opportunities in LA's short film community.
notes: Festival runs August 2026 in Hollywood. Short scripts and TV pilots only.

name: BlueCat Screenplay Competition
entryFeeNote: USD $55 — includes written feedback on your script
description: Founded in 1998 by screenwriter Gordy Hoffman, BlueCat is particularly valued for the written feedback every entrant receives on their script — not just finalists. Accepts feature screenplays and short scripts. Open internationally, with a strong reputation for nurturing emerging writers rather than simply awarding the already-established.
notes: All entrants receive written script feedback. Open internationally.

name: ScreenCraft Genre Competitions
entryFeeNote: USD $40–$55 depending on category and deadline
description: Runs year-round genre-specific competitions including Drama, Comedy, Horror, Sci-Fi, Animation, and TV Pilot, each judged by relevant industry professionals. Known for high-quality judging panels and strong finalist-to-representation conversion. Open to international writers including Australians.
notes: Multiple competitions run at different times of year. Check site for current open genres.

name: BAFTA Rocliffe New Writing Competition
description: Backed by BAFTA and run by Rocliffe, this competition gives emerging UK writers the chance to have their work read by top film and TV executives. Winning scripts receive a live industry showcase reading at a BAFTA event. Highly prestigious within the UK screen industry and a recognised launchpad for new voices.
notes: UK writers. Check Rocliffe site for current open category (Film, TV Drama, Comedy).

name: BFI Network x BAFTA Crew
description: A joint BFI and BAFTA professional development programme for emerging UK filmmakers and screenwriters. Selected participants gain access to masterclasses, peer networking, and one-on-one industry introductions. One of the UK's most respected pathways for early-career screen talent.
notes: UK-based emerging talent only. Applications open periodically — check BFI Network.

name: Channel 4 screenwriting courses and competitions
description: Channel 4 runs recurring screenwriting talent schemes in partnership with ScreenSkills, including the 4Stories initiative and writer attachment programmes. Selected writers may receive paid placements on productions and direct commissioning opportunities. One of the UK's most direct routes from competition to professional credit.
notes: UK writers. Schemes vary year to year — check Channel 4 Producers site for current openings.
